recipes.title: Coq au Vin
recipes.description: Burgundian chicken braised in red wine with bacon, mushrooms and pearl onions.
steps: Marinate the chicken in the wine with herbs and garlic for several hours, then drain and pat dry.
Render the bacon lardons in a Dutch oven, remove and reserve.
Brown the chicken pieces in the bacon fat, season and dust with flour.
Pour over the marinade and stock, add tomato paste, and braise covered for 60 minutes.
Sauté the pearl onions and mushrooms in butter until golden, then add to the pot for the last 15 minutes.
Reduce the sauce if needed, return the bacon, and serve with potatoes or noodles.

recipes.title: Boeuf Bourguignon
recipes.description: Slow-braised beef in red wine with bacon, mushrooms and pearl onions.
steps: Render the bacon in a heavy pot, remove and reserve.
Brown the seasoned beef in batches in the bacon fat.
Sauté the diced onion, carrot and garlic, then dust with flour.
Pour in the wine and stock, add tomato paste and thyme, return the beef and bacon.
Cover and braise at 160°C for 2.5 hours until the beef is fork tender.
Sauté the pearl onions and mushrooms separately and stir into the stew before serving.

recipes.title: Ratatouille
recipes.description: Provençal stew of summer vegetables: aubergine, courgette, pepper and tomato.
steps: Dice the aubergine, courgette, peppers and onion into bite-size pieces.
Sauté each vegetable separately in olive oil until lightly coloured, then combine.
Add chopped tomatoes, garlic and thyme, season and simmer 30 minutes.
The vegetables should be tender but still hold their shape.
Stir in torn basil and a final drizzle of olive oil.
Serve warm or at room temperature with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Cassoulet
recipes.description: Languedoc bean stew with duck confit, sausage and pork, baked under a crust.
steps: Soak the beans overnight, then cook in fresh water with aromatics until just tender.
Brown the pork and sausages in duck fat, then sauté the onion, carrot and garlic.
Combine pork, beans, tomato paste, herbs and stock in a large casserole.
Tuck in the duck legs and sausages and simmer 90 minutes.
Top with breadcrumbs and bake at 160°C for 1 hour, breaking the crust into the stew twice.
Rest 10 minutes and serve with red wine.

recipes.title: Quiche Lorraine
recipes.description: Buttery pastry filled with smoky lardons, cream and egg custard.
steps: Roll out the pastry and line a tart tin, then chill 30 minutes.
Blind-bake at 190°C for 15 minutes with weights, then 5 minutes uncovered.
Render the lardons until golden and scatter over the base.
Whisk eggs, cream, milk, nutmeg and seasoning, then pour into the case.
Sprinkle with Gruyère if using and bake at 170°C for 30 minutes until just set.
Cool slightly before slicing so the custard sets.

recipes.title: Bouillabaisse
recipes.description: Marseille fish stew with rascasse, conger, fennel, saffron and rouille.
steps: Sweat the fennel, leek and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add tomatoes, orange peel, saffron and Pernod and cook 10 minutes.
Pour in the fish stock and simmer 20 minutes, then strain if you want a refined broth.
Add firm-fleshed fish first and simmer 5 minutes, then add delicate fish for 4 minutes more.
Season carefully and serve with toasted baguette and a spoonful of rouille on top.
Pass extra rouille and grated Gruyère at the table.

recipes.title: Crème Brûlée
recipes.description: Vanilla custard topped with a thin shell of caramelised sugar.
steps: Heat the cream with the split vanilla pod until just steaming and infuse 10 minutes.
Whisk yolks with caster sugar until pale, then pour the warm cream over, whisking.
Strain into ramekins and place in a roasting tin filled with hot water halfway up.
Bake at 140°C for about 35 minutes until just set with a slight wobble.
Chill at least 4 hours, then sprinkle with demerara sugar.
Caramelise with a torch until deep amber and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Tarte Tatin
recipes.description: Upside-down caramelised apple tart on buttery puff pastry.
steps: Peel, core and halve the apples; toss with lemon juice.
Make a dry caramel with the sugar in an oven-safe pan, then stir in the butter.
Pack the apple halves cut-side up into the caramel.
Cook on the stove 15 minutes until the apples soften.
Cover with the pastry, tucking the edges down, and bake at 200°C for 25 minutes.
Rest 5 minutes, then carefully invert onto a plate so the caramel coats the apples.

recipes.title: Croque Monsieur
recipes.description: Grilled ham and Gruyère sandwich smothered in béchamel.
steps: Make a thick béchamel with butter, flour and milk; finish with mustard and nutmeg.
Spread mustard on the bread, layer ham and a little Gruyère, and close the sandwiches.
Top each sandwich with béchamel and a generous handful of cheese.
Bake at 200°C for 12 minutes, then grill 2 minutes until bubbling and golden.
Serve hot, ideally with a small green salad.

recipes.title: Salade Niçoise
recipes.description: Provençal composed salad with tuna, eggs, olives, anchovies and beans.
steps: Whisk the olive oil, vinegar and seasoning into a vinaigrette.
Arrange potatoes, beans and tomatoes on a wide platter.
Top with flaked tuna, halved eggs, olives and anchovies.
Scatter sliced red onion over the top.
Drizzle generously with the vinaigrette and serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Sole Meunière
recipes.description: Whole sole dredged in flour, panfried in foaming butter with lemon.
steps: Season the soles and dredge in flour, shaking off the excess.
Melt half the butter in a large pan until foaming.
Cook the soles 3 minutes per side until golden and just cooked through.
Transfer the fish to warm plates.
Add the remaining butter to the pan and cook to a nut-brown beurre noisette, then add lemon juice and parsley.
Pour the sauce over the fish and serve at once.

recipes.title: Confit de Canard
recipes.description: Duck legs slow-cooked in their own fat until tender, then crisped.
steps: Rub the duck legs with salt, garlic, thyme and pepper and refrigerate overnight.
Rinse the legs and pat dry, then place in a deep pan with bay leaves.
Cover completely with melted duck fat and warm to a bare simmer.
Cook at 90°C for 3 hours until the meat almost falls from the bone.
Cool the legs in the fat for storage; before serving crisp the skin in a hot pan.
Serve with sautéed potatoes cooked in a little of the duck fat.

recipes.title: Soufflé au Fromage
recipes.description: Cheese soufflé that rises tall and golden from a Gruyère béchamel base.
steps: Butter a soufflé dish and dust with Parmigiano.
Make a thick béchamel with butter, flour and milk and season with nutmeg.
Off the heat stir in Gruyère and the egg yolks one by one.
Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold into the warm base in three additions.
Pour into the dish and bake at 190°C for 25 minutes without opening the oven.
Serve immediately while puffed and golden.

recipes.title: Pot-au-Feu
recipes.description: French boiled beef and vegetables in a clear, savoury broth.
steps: Place the beef and bones in a large pot, cover with cold water and bring to a boil; skim carefully.
Add the studded onion, bouquet garni and salt, then simmer gently 2 hours.
Add the carrots, leeks, turnips and celery and cook another hour until tender.
Slice the meat and arrange with the vegetables on a warm platter.
Strain the broth, skim the fat, and serve in a bowl alongside.
Pass mustard, gherkins and coarse salt at the table.

recipes.title: Blanquette de Veau
recipes.description: Veal stew in a creamy white sauce with mushrooms and pearl onions.
steps: Blanch the veal in boiling water 5 minutes, drain and rinse.
Place the veal back in the pot with carrots, leek, studded onion and bouquet garni; cover with water.
Simmer 90 minutes until tender, skimming as needed.
Cook the pearl onions and mushrooms in butter separately.
Make a roux with butter and flour, then whisk in the strained cooking liquid.
Off the heat, finish with cream, yolk and lemon juice; combine with veal and vegetables and warm gently.

recipes.title: Moules Marinière
recipes.description: Mussels steamed open in white wine with shallot, parsley and butter.
steps: Discard any open mussels that don't close when tapped.
Sweat shallots, garlic and thyme in butter without colour.
Pour in the wine and bring to a vigorous boil.
Add the mussels, cover and cook 4-5 minutes, shaking the pot until they open.
Discard any unopened mussels, scatter with parsley and serve in deep bowls with frites.

recipes.title: Steak Frites
recipes.description: Pan-seared steak with crisp double-cooked fries and herb butter.
steps: Cut the potatoes into thick fries, soak in water, then dry well.
Blanch the fries at 140°C until soft, drain and cool.
Sear the steaks in a smoking hot pan 3 minutes per side, basting with butter.
Rest the steaks 5 minutes while you fry the chips at 180°C until golden and crisp.
Make a quick herb butter with the shallot, parsley and thyme.
Serve the steaks topped with herb butter and a generous pile of frites.

recipes.title: Soupe à l'Oignon
recipes.description: Caramelised onion soup topped with toasted baguette and Gruyère.
steps: Cook the onions in butter and oil over low heat with the sugar for 45 minutes until deeply caramelised.
Deglaze with white wine and let it reduce.
Add the stock and thyme and simmer 30 minutes.
Toast the baguette slices until golden.
Ladle the soup into bowls, top with baguette and a generous handful of Gruyère.
Grill until the cheese is bubbling and golden, then serve at once.

recipes.title: Madeleines
recipes.description: Shell-shaped sponge cakes with a humped middle, made famous by Proust.
steps: Whisk eggs and sugar until pale and ribbon-like.
Sift in flour and baking powder and fold gently.
Mix in the melted butter, lemon zest and honey.
Rest the batter in the fridge for at least 1 hour.
Spoon into a buttered madeleine mould and bake at 220°C for 4 minutes, then 180°C for 5 minutes.
Unmould while warm so the shell is crisp.

recipes.title: Pain Perdu
recipes.description: French toast made with brioche, soaked in egg custard and fried in butter.
steps: Whisk eggs, milk, cream, sugar and vanilla in a wide dish.
Soak the brioche slices in the custard for a minute on each side.
Melt butter in a frying pan over medium heat.
Fry the soaked slices 2 minutes per side until golden and just set inside.
Dust with icing sugar and serve immediately with fresh berries or maple syrup.

recipes.title: Choucroute Garnie
recipes.description: Alsatian sauerkraut braised with Riesling, sausages and smoked pork.
steps: Rinse the sauerkraut and squeeze dry.
Soften the onion in duck fat in a large pot.
Add the sauerkraut, juniper, bay, wine and water to cover.
Bury the smoked pork in the kraut and simmer covered for 90 minutes.
Add sausages and potatoes and cook another 40 minutes.
Pile the sauerkraut on a platter and arrange the meats and potatoes around it.

recipes.title: Pissaladière
recipes.description: Niçoise onion tart with anchovies and black olives on a thin base.
steps: Cook the onions slowly in olive oil with thyme and garlic for an hour until very soft and golden.
Roll the bread dough into a thin rectangle on an oiled tray.
Spread the cooked onions over the base.
Make a lattice of anchovy fillets and tuck olives into the diamonds.
Bake at 220°C for 18 minutes until the base is crisp and golden.
Serve warm or at room temperature, sliced into squares.

recipes.title: Gratin Dauphinois
recipes.description: Sliced potatoes baked slowly in cream and garlic until silky.
steps: Rub a baking dish with garlic and butter.
Slice the potatoes thinly on a mandoline.
Layer the potatoes in the dish, seasoning each layer with salt, pepper and nutmeg.
Warm the cream and milk and pour over the potatoes.
Bake at 160°C for 75 minutes until tender and golden, pressing down occasionally.
Rest 10 minutes so the cream sets, then serve.

recipes.title: Croissant
recipes.description: Buttery laminated pastry with crisp shell and tender, layered crumb.
steps: Mix flour, milk, yeast, sugar and salt to a smooth dough; chill 1 hour.
Pound the cold butter into a thin square between sheets of parchment.
Roll the dough out, encase the butter, and complete three letter folds with rests in between.
Roll the laminated dough out, cut into long triangles and roll into croissants.
Prove for 2 hours until visibly puffed, then brush with egg.
Bake at 200°C for 18 minutes until deeply golden and shatteringly crisp.

recipes.title: Macarons
recipes.description: Delicate almond meringue sandwiches with smooth ganache or buttercream.
steps: Sift ground almonds and icing sugar together; combine with half the egg whites to form a paste.
Cook caster sugar and water to 118°C, then pour into the remaining whites whipped to soft peaks.
Whisk the meringue until cool and glossy, then fold into the almond paste in three additions.
Pipe small rounds onto trays and rest 30 minutes until a skin forms.
Bake at 150°C for 14 minutes; cool completely.
Sandwich with chocolate ganache made from melted chocolate and warm cream; mature 24 hours before serving.
